advice: Rest, stay hydrated, and use over-the-counter decongestants or lozenges as needed. Symptoms usually resolve within 7-10 days.
Rest, drink fluids, and take fever-reducing medication like acetaminophen. See a doctor if fever is high or lasts more than 3 days.
Rest in a dark, quiet room, apply a cold compress, and consider an over-the-counter pain reliever. Consult a doctor for frequent or severe migraines.
Try relaxation techniques, stay hydrated, and take a mild pain reliever if needed. Persistent headaches should be evaluated by a doctor.
Avoid known allergens, consider an antihistamine, and use saline nasal spray. See a doctor if symptoms are persistent or severe.
Stay hydrated with small sips of water or oral rehydration solution, and rest. Seek care if there are signs of severe dehydration or blood in stool.
Drink plenty of water and see a doctor soon, as a UTI typically requires antibiotics for proper treatment.
Drink water or an electrolyte solution steadily throughout the day and rest. Seek care if dizziness is severe or persistent.
Follow the RICE method: rest, ice, compression, and elevation. See a doctor if you cannot bear weight or swelling is severe.
Maintain a consistent sleep schedule, limit screen time before bed, and avoid caffeine late in the day. Consult a doctor if it persists beyond a few weeks.
Try slow breathing exercises, reduce caffeine intake, and consider talking to a mental health professional if symptoms persist.
Cool the area under running water for 10-15 minutes, keep it clean, and avoid ice or butter. Seek care for large or blistering burns.
Rest briefly, apply ice then heat, and do gentle stretching. See a doctor if pain radiates down the leg or persists beyond a couple of weeks.
Gargle with warm salt water, drink warm fluids, and use throat lozenges. See a doctor if it persists more than a week or is accompanied by high fever.
